subject: math
source_title: New General Mathematics SSS 1 by M.F. Macrae et al
learning_objectives: Construct quadrilaterals (trapezium, parallelogram) using ruler and compasses
Describe and construct the locus of points at a given distance from a fixed point
Describe and construct the locus of points equidistant from two given points
Describe and construct the locus of points equidistant from two intersecting lines

sections.heading: Construction of Quadrilaterals
sections.content: To construct a parallelogram PQRS with QR = 5cm, RS = 11cm and angle QRS = 135°: (1) draw QR = 5cm; (2) construct 135° at R; (3) mark S at 11cm; (4) with S as centre (radius 5cm) and Q as centre (radius 11cm), draw arcs intersecting at P; (5) join SP and QP. The shorter diagonal can be measured from the completed diagram.

sections.heading: Construction of Equilateral Triangles
sections.content: An equilateral triangle has all sides equal and all angles equal to 60°. To construct equilateral triangle XYZ with XY = 5cm: (1) draw XY = 5cm; (2) with centres X and Y and radius 5cm, draw arcs intersecting at Z; (3) join XZ and YZ. Note: the angle bisectors of all three angles meet at a single point inside the triangle (the incentre).

sections.heading: Locus of Points — Fixed Distance from a Point
sections.content: The locus of all points at a fixed distance x from a point O is a circle of radius x centred at O.

sections.heading: Locus of Points Equidistant from Two Points
sections.content: The locus of points equidistant from two fixed points X and Y is the perpendicular bisector of the line segment XY.

sections.heading: Locus of Points Equidistant from a Line
sections.content: The locus of points at a fixed distance x from a straight line AB (extending in both directions) consists of two straight lines parallel to AB, each at distance x from it. In two dimensions, this locus is two separate parallel lines.

sections.heading: Locus of Points Equidistant from Two Intersecting Lines
sections.content: The locus of points equidistant from two intersecting lines AB and CD is the pair of angle bisectors of the angles formed at their intersection point. The two bisectors are perpendicular to each other.
